---
number-sections: false
---

```{=latex}
% Quarto auto-emits \chapter*{Dedication} at the top of this file. We then use
% a TikZ overlay anchored to (current page.center) to place the italic text at
% the exact vertical mid-line of the page, independent of the chapter heading's
% spacing. This is the MIT Press dedication convention: heading at top of page,
% epigraph block optically centered.
\thispagestyle{empty}
\begin{tikzpicture}[remember picture, overlay]
\node[anchor=center, align=center, text width=0.72\paperwidth]
  at (current page.center)
  {\itshape
   For my wife and my children,\\
   who gave me the time this book required\\
   and never asked for it back.};
\end{tikzpicture}
\null\clearpage
```

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}

# Dedication {.unnumbered}

*For my wife and my children, who gave me the time this book required and never asked for it back.*

:::
